response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a versatile full-stack and backend engineer with genuine distributed systems exposure (high-throughput microservices at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby) and a solid software craftsmanship ethos, but he is a poor fit for this role. The single most disqualifying gap is C++: the role requires 8 years of C++ experience, yet his resume shows only a ~6-month engagement at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project using the language — C++ does not even appear in his skills list. Additionally, his career arc is firmly in web/application engineering (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, Ember.js), with no evidence of production cloud platform architecture, compute/storage infrastructure ownership, or formal technical leadership of infrastructure teams.
strengths: Hands-on distributed systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, building low-latency services handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Demonstrated software craftsmanship culture (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) and community knowledge-sharing through workshops and conference talks
Broad polyglot background and 'staff' seniority signals capacity to operate across system boundaries and mentor peers
concerns: Critically insufficient C++ experience (~6 months vs. the 8-year minimum requirement); language is absent from his skills list entirely
No cloud platform or compute infrastructure leadership — cloud exposure is limited to AWS CDK usage within a product team, not platform ownership or architecture

Career trajectory is web/application-layer focused (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, Ember.js), with no evidence of working on operating systems, hypervisors, networking stacks, storage, or hardware architecture
